sessions.lever: ALLOCDBG-diagnosed RA-priority flip via var_s7 split-init (refs 3->5) + hdr split-stage (sched1 nor hoist)

reviewer.evidence: Change #2 (hdr split-stage) PASSes independently — live intermediate, defensible C. Change #1 (var_s7 = arg0; var_s7 += temp_v0;) is BORDERLINE: same-variable split-init accumulation with the explicit, only purpose of inflating reg_n_refs from 3 to 5 (priority 329->1075, formula log2(refs)*refs/livelen at global.c:611) so var_s7 wins $s7 over var_fp. Intermediate `var_s7 == arg0` IS used by the subsequent `+=`, so NOT a dead store (Lever D doesn't apply). SOTN sanctions different-variable routing (`randy = basePoint.x; baseX = randy;`) and no-op self-assignment (`idxSub = idxSub;`); does NOT directly cite same-variable split-init for refs manipulation. Reviewer cannot independently resolve whether this is a new spelling of sanctioned variable-reuse or outside the boundary. User policy ruling required.

remaining_gap.summary: RA-priority tie (refs=3, livelen 91 vs 73) — var_fp shorter-life wins $s7, var_s7 gets $fp (6 reg-swap diffs); sched1 places `nor v0,zero,s0` AFTER move/addiu pair at .L80049D14 (2 diffs). Total 8 honest pure-C diffs vs target.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD carries 10 regfix subst rules — 7 for s7<->$fp register-rename swap (idx 17/19/20/72/88/94/110) + 3 for nor placement reorder at idx 60/61/62. If user PASSes the split-init technique, candidate retires ALL 10 rules to COMPLETED-C; oracle SHA1 == target.

next_hypotheses: USER POLICY: Does the same-variable split-init-accumulation pattern (`x = a; x += b;` replacing `x = a + b;`) fall within the SOTN-sanctioned variable-reuse-for-codegen-control category? PASS -> commit candidate.c as Match: func_80049C24, retire 10 rules. FAIL -> back to lever search.

If user rules split-init OUT-OF-SCOPE: investigate Lever A (block-local var split) variants — try fresh-local at var_fp's use sites to SHRINK var_fp's livelen below var_s7's (currently 73<91; need >91 to flip priority). Requires arg0+v0 to be saveable across calls.

If still no legit lever after Lever A exploration: try directed permuter from candidate (PERM_VAR_REPLACE on var_s7/var_fp init), or m2c-reconstruct target's structure for hints (m2c not installed in this venv — install needed).

rejected_forms.form: v1: swap declaration order of var_s7/var_fp (declare var_fp first)
rejected_forms.reason: No effect on RA priority — declaration order doesn't shift pseudo numbers or livelens enough

rejected_forms.form: v2: swap assignment order (compute var_fp before var_s7)
rejected_forms.reason: No effect — both pseudos still tied refs=3, var_fp livelen still shorter

rejected_forms.form: v3: inline `arg0 + temp_v0` at tslGlobalMemFree call sites
rejected_forms.reason: Much worse — forces arg0 and temp_v0 to be saved as long-life callee-saves; cascade of register pressure breaks scheduling

rejected_forms.form: v4: inline `arg0 + v0` at func_800520B8 call sites (parallel to v3 for var_fp)
v5: block-scoped declarations with combined initializers
rejected_forms.reason: Block scope in C doesn't actually shrink RTL livelen (pseudos created based on actual uses, not declaration scope)

rejected_forms.form: v6: reuse temp_v0 to hold var_s7's value (`temp_v0 = arg0 + temp_v0;`)
rejected_forms.reason: Does flip RA correctly (refs jumps to 5, $s7 allocation matches target) BUT breaks scheduling: target wants `lw $v0, 4($v0); addu $s7, $a0, $v0` (load + add) and v6 emits `lw $s7, 4($v1); addu $s7, $s7, $a0` (direct load into target reg + self-add) — different bytes

rejected_forms.form: v11: `register` keyword on var_s7 (no asm pin)
rejected_forms.reason: MIPS GCC 2.7.2's global.c doesn't check DECL_REGISTER for RA priority; keyword is inert
